era_context: Early Dynastic Period in Mesopotamia (c. 3150–2334 BCE); Egypt's First Dynasty (c. 3100–2890 BCE). The Indus Valley Civilization is in its early phases. Writing systems are nascent or recently developed in Mesopotamia and Egypt.

events.title: Mesopotamian Early Dynastic I period
events.description: Sumerian city-states continued to develop complex administrative and religious institutions. This year falls within the Early Dynastic I phase (c. 3150–2900 BCE), when the first true cities with monumental architecture, writing systems, and organized priesthoods emerged. No specific events are recorded for 3111 BCE.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Uruk, Lagash, Nippur, and other Sumerian sites

events.certainty_note: Dating is based on stratigraphic sequences and pottery typology. Absolute dating in this era is uncertain by ±50–100 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The year 3111 BCE has no attested specific events; it is a placeholder within the Early Dynastic period.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 3111 BCE is entirely archaeological. No contemporary written records exist. The chronology is based on stratigraphic sequences and pottery typology. The surviving sources are heavily weighted toward elite contexts.
